description: Comparison of our matchers against external SOTA baselines

analysis.ic_vs_deeprare: IC Resnik Recall@1 (65.0%) exceeds DeepRare HPO-only (64.4%) by 0.6pp. DeepRare multimodal (70.6%) is higher but uses additional gene/variant data we lack.
analysis.ic_vs_phenobrain: IC Resnik Top-10 (80.3%) exceeds PhenoBrain standalone (65.4%) and approaches human+computer (81.3%). Strong result for a pure IC approach.
analysis.freq_ic_regression: Freq-IC underperforms IC by 17.4pp on Top-1. Orphanet frequency weighting may over-penalize via excluded terms or dilute signal for diseases with mostly frequent annotations.
analysis.graph_model_gaps: Node2vec and GAT require better training (more walks, larger dims, proper hyperparameter tuning). GAT needs harder negative sampling for the dense disease-phenotype bipartite structure. Gene data integration (after genemap2 fix) may also help.
improvement_opportunities: Gene data fusion: integrate genemap2 gene-disease edges (after parser fix)
Better node2vec: dims=128, walks=10, length=30, epochs=5 (needs more RAM/time)
GAT improvements: harder negatives, edge-type-aware attention, larger model
LLM-based HPO extraction: use clinical notes to expand phenotype profiles
Ensemble: train weights on held-out set instead of uniform averaging
